sample_tweets.text: 싸이코가 김정은대통령님,북한,피해자 최 상대로 폭행 하는 놈이 있는데 지속적 북한 위협 한다면 지금 김정은대통령님,북한경찰이 반려견 폭행 하는 사이버 놈, 가족을 모두 잡아서 처형시켜주세요. 오늘 북한에서 그놈의 집, 모든 재산을 처분 해 주세요 그 놈의 사이버 폭행 죽여야 멈출 것 입니다

선거 정의와 주권 회복을 위한 시민 선언 인류의 역사에서, 자유로운 시민이 자신들의 선거 과정을 부패하게 만드는 만연하고 음험한 조작을 떨쳐내고, 자신들의 진정하고 오염되지 않은 의지에 의해 선택된 정부를 가질 불가침의 권리를 되찾는 것이 필요해질 때, 인류의 판단에 대한 엄숙한 경의는 그들로 하여금 이러한 단절을 결행하게 만든 원인과 그들의 자유를 지키기 위해 필요한 개혁조치들을 선언하도록 요구한다.  우리는 다음과 같은 진리를 자명한 것으로 믿는다. 모든 시민은 창조주로부터 교육 수준이나 재산에 상관없이 모든 시민이 자유롭고, 공정하며, 단순하고, 투명하며, 검증 가능한 선거를 통해 자신들의 대표를 선출할 권리를 부여받았다. 투표라는 신성한 행위를 통해 표현되는 피통치자의 동의만이 정부 권력의 유일하고 정당한 원천이다. 국내외를 막론하고 어떤 시스템, 기술, 또는 음모 세력이 사기, 강압, 복잡성, 또는 은폐를 통해 이러한 동의를 전복할 때, 그러한 폭정을 거부하고 명확성, 접근성, 그리고 대중의 감시에 뿌리를 둔 개혁을 수립하는 것은 시민의 권리이자 의무이다. 시민들의 철저한 감시 하에 손으로 기표하고 손으로 개표하는 종이 투표로 치러지는 선거의 정직성은 자유 공화국의 초석이며, 그 어떤 기계나 관료 기구, 책임지지 않는 세력도 시민과 시민의 목소리 사이에 끼어들 수 없다.  최근 이 미국에서 자행된 선거 관행의 역사는 유권자의 신성한 표를 침해하고 시민의 의지를 찬탈하기 위해 고안된 끊임없는 가해의 역사이다. 이를 증명하기 위해, 편견 없는 전 세계 앞에 다음과 같은 사실들을 엄숙히 제출하는 바이다.  유권자 명부의 임의 삭제, 투표소에서의 위협, 투표 시간 및 장소의 오도, 그리고 소수자와 취약계층을 겨냥한 장벽 설치를 통해 적격 유권자의 투표를 억압하고 그들의 목소리를 침묵시키려 획책하였다.  허위 신분 생성, 사망자 또는 가공의 인물 등록, 그리고 허술한 신원 확인을 악용하여 유령 투표를 주입함으로써 정당한 시민들의 의지를 희석시켰다.  물리적 또는 디지털 방식을 가리지 않고 투표용지를 무단 투입, 수거(harvesting), 폐기, 또는 변조하였으며, 이는 대개 관리 연속성(chain of custody)의 훼손이나 날조된 비상사태를 통해 은폐되었다.  해킹, 악성코드, 백도어, 또는 위조 장비를 통해 전자 투표 시스템을 조작함으로써, 선거 기계를 신뢰할 수 없게 만들고 감지되지 않는 부정행위에 취약하도록 방치하였다.  유권자, 투표소 종사자, 그리고 선거 관료들을 강압하고 매수하여 그들의 궁핍함이나 탐욕을 악용하였으며, 공모한 법 집행기관과 법원은 가해자들이 책임을 지지 않도록 비호하였다.  지연된 집계, 기록 조작, 감사 방해, 그리고 검증 절차의 재정의를 통해 결과를 허위로 보고함으로써 불일치와 부정 정황을 은폐하였다.  국내외의 불순한 세력이 유권자 데이터베이스와 시스템에 대한 조작 및 사이버 공격을 감행하도록 침투 방치함으로써, 우리 민주적 절차의 주권을 훼손하였다.  다크 머니(음성 자금), 불법 자금, 그리고 위장 단체를 동원하여 선거 운동을 왜곡하고, 자원을 탕진시키며, 진정한 개혁 노력으로부터 시민들을 오도하였다.  허위 정보, 심리전, 딥페이크, 그리고 미디어 조작을 배치하여 유권자들을 혼란에 빠뜨리고, 낙담시키거나 기만함으로써 분열과 불신을 조장하였다.  부패한 관료, 침투된 풀뿌리 조직, 그리고 정권 연장을 위한 법률전(lawfare), 게리맨더링, 법원 장악 등 조작된 사법 절차를 통한 내부의 위협으로 시민의 의지에 반하여 권력을 고착화하였다.  법적 모호성, 허술한 감시, 중앙집권적 통제, 그리고 책임지지 않는 기술을 체계적으로 악용하여 효율성이라는 가면 아래 부정이 자라기 좋은 비옥한 토양을 제공하였다.  복잡한 전자 투표 기계에 의존함으로써 개표 과정을 불투명하게 만들고, 투명성을 결여시켰으며, 선거를 조작에 노출시켜 시민들을 직접적인 감시로부터 소외시켰다.  유권자 등록과 선거 관리의 중앙집권화를 통해 지역의 통제권을 박탈하고, 책임지지 않는 기관들이 결과를 좌지우지할 수 있도록 권한을 부여하였다.  독점적 권리라는 핑계, 과도한 수수료, 또는 보존 기간에 대한 자의적 해석을 구실로 투표소별 집계 기록(cast vote records) 및 관리 연속성 문서와 같은 선거 기록에 대한 대중의 접근을 차단하였다.  부재자 투표의 안전한 제한에 실패하여, 철저한 조사 없이 비밀성과 정직성을 보장할 수 없는 검증 불가능한 투표용지를 양산함으로써 부정의 빌미를 제공하였다.  이러한 억압의 모든 단계에서 우리는 탄원, 소송, 대중 토론, 그리고 지치지 않는 옹호 활동을 통해 시정을 요구했으나, 돌아온 것은 거부와 지연, 혹은 우리의 헌정 공화국을 흔들려 한다는 모함뿐이었다. 우리는 유권자 등록의 카운티(지방자치구) 이관, 부재자 투표 제한, 소규모 투표구에서의 단일일 투표 실시, 그리고 대중의 감시 하에 손으로 기표한 종이 투표지 개표 등 신뢰를 회복하기 위한 개혁안을 제시해 왔다. 우리는 수개표 선거를 옹호하는 시민들의 노력을 통해, 단순하고 투명한 방법이 실행 가능할 뿐만 아니라 우리의 선거 과정을 되찾는 데 필수적임을 증명해 보였다. 그러나 권력을 잡은 자들은 우리의 간청을 묵살하고, 투명성을 방해하며, 시민의 의지를 가리는 시스템을 고집해 왔다. 이러한 폐해를 허용하고, 투표의 관리를 불투명한 기계와 책임지지 않는 관료 기구에 위임하는 시스템은 자유 국가를 봉사하기에 부적합하다.  우리는 결코 침묵 속에 주저앉아 있지 않았다. 우리는 워크숍, 시위, 대중 감사를 통해 동료 시민들을 교육했고, 부패한 투표 기록과 조작된 유권자 명부 같은 추문들을 폭로했다. 우리는 선거가 1,500명 이하의 시민으로 구성된 소규모 투표구에서 치러져야 하며, 고화질 비디오 감시 하에 상호 투명한 보관함에 투입된 종이 투표지를 양당 시민들의 수작업으로 개표하고 검증할 것을 촉구해 왔다. 우리는 각 카운티가 대면 확인 및 증인 서명이 포함된 종이 등록 카드를 통해 유권자 등록의 독점적 관리권을 되찾고, 대중이 상호 참조할 수 있도록 범용 포맷의 읽기 전용 유권자 명부를 발행할 것을 강력히 요구했다. 우리는 부재자 투표를 증명된 필요가 있는 이들로 엄격히 제한하고 조작을 방지하기 위해 정밀하게 심사할 것과, 단계적 조작을 차단하기 위해 단일 국가 투표일을 지정할 것을 주장해 왔다. 그러나 이러한 노력들은 저항과 관료적 타성, 혹은 불신을 조장한다는 비난에 부딪혔고, 결국 우리에게 이 부패한 시스템으로부터의 독립을 선언하는 것 외에는 다른 선택지를 남겨두지 않았다.  그러므로 우리 미국 시민들은 엄숙히 집회하여, 우리 의도의 정당함을 세계의 최고 재판장이신 신에게 호소하며, 시민들의 이름과 권능으로 우리의 선거 조작의 폭정으로부터 자유로워야 함을 엄숙히 공표하고 선언한다. 우리는 우리의 의지를 가리는 복잡하고 신뢰할 수 없는 기계와 중앙집권적 시스템을 거부하며, 선거가 다음과 같이 치러질 것을 요구한다.  유권자 등록은 카운티의 독점적 관리 하에 두며, 2~4년마다 증인의 서명이 날인된 종이 등록 카드를 사용하여 대면으로 적격을 확인하고, 투표구별로 보관한다. 또한, 무결성을 보장하기 위해 전국 고유 유권자 번호와 함께 대중이 상호 참조할 수 있도록 읽기 전용 명부로 발행되어야 한다.  부재자 투표 제한은 증명된 필요가 있는 이들로 엄격히 제한하고, 철저한 심사를 거쳐야 하며, 군인과 일반 유권자를 명확히 구분하여 식별한다. 비밀성을 침해하지 않으면서 무결성을 높이기 위해 디지털 투표 해시(hash)로 검증할 수 있다.  전국 공휴일 단일일 투표는 전국적으로 표준화된 시작 및 종료 시간을 적용하여 시행하며, 연속적인 비디오 감시 하에 투명하고 잠겨 있는 투표함에 손으로 기표한 종이 투표지를 넣는 방식으로 1,500명 이하의 소규모 투표구에서 진행된다.  수개표 투표는 투표가 실시된 바로 그 자리에서 주 정부가 승인한 방식에 따라 양당 시민들과 참관인들에 의해 집계되어야 하며, 결과는 투표구 외부에 즉시 게시된다. 투표지 이미지, 집계표, 관리 연속성 문서, 영상 등 모든 기록은 모든 시민이 검증할 수 있도록 카운티 웹사이트에 전면 공개되어야 한다.  대중 감사는 모든 시민에게 개방되어야 한다. 여기에는 6~12학년 학생들을 시민 의식 교육의 일환으로 참여시켜 투표지를 재집계하고, 결과를 비교하며, 표준화된 카운티 양식을 통해 불일치를 보고하도록 하는 것이 포함된다. 심화 과정의 학생들은 비자연적인 통계 패턴을 분석할 수 있으며, 이 모든 과정은 민주적 참여 행위로서 장려되고 축하받아야 한다.  우리는 우리의 동의를 배신하는 절차에 맹종하는 것으로부터 벗어났음을 선언하며, 선거를 개혁하고, 지역의 통제권을 회복하고, 모든 기록에 대한 철저한 대중의 접근을 보장하며, 사기와 강압 및 개입에 맞서 철통같은 방어벽을 구축할 권능이 우리에게 있음을 단언한다. 이 선언을 지지하고 신성한 섭리의 보호를 굳건히 신뢰하며, 우리는 서로의 생명과 재산, 그리고 신성한 명예를 걸고 상호 맹세한다.  이제 이것을 사방에 공유하는 것은 당신의 몫입니다. 충분한 사람들이 이것을 본다면, 우리는 필요한 변화를 만들어낼 수 있습니다. 공유하고, 공유하고, 공유하십시오! nitter.tiekoetter.com/PatriotMarkCook/status…

The Loop-Gated OAM Pressure Engine has been updated with a public forensic entry layer.  Live Space: huggingface.co/spaces/HirMod… What it is:  A local, browser-based forensic evidence instrument for reverse-backtrace pressure reading and bounded review receipts.  Workflow:  Drop evidence → read local metadata → reverse-map the structure → surface pressure points → bound the claim → export a review receipt  It is built for PE/EXE/driver evidence, Linux/ELF/module evidence, ZIP/package chains, incident notes, telemetry exports, and forensic handoff review. What it does not do: No uploads. No live execution. No live kernel access. No automatic compromise proof. No external certification or endorsement claim. The point is not to replace DFIR expertise. The point is to help preserve evidence structure, uncertainty, claim boundaries, and review routes before partial signals get turned into false certainty. This version also includes the Primordial Lexicon Bridge layer, so the same forensic readout can be viewed through:  Plain English Technical Systems C2PA / Provenance Linux / POSIX Windows / Microsoft Red-Team Boundary Original HIR/OAM  Same evidence. Same route. Same receipt. Different explanation lens.  Built and developed by Collin D. Weber.

Don’t get me started on my “crime scene photo” soap box — my time in DFIR taught me most orgs don’t even have enough infrastructure in place to even know what happened.   The whole point is to govern before execution. And that’s the real unlock: operating within declared guardrails isn’t just safer, it throws off efficiency benefits as a byproduct and de-risks the move to autonomous operations.  Those three you named—allowed outcome, acceptable tradeoffs, what uncertainty triggers escalation—are the actual primitives.   Making them real takes more first-class machinery than people expect: a sufficiency layer for “how much certainty is enough,” risk-weighted handling when claims compete, and a hard gate that won’t let an agent act on anything stale or contested. That’s the thing we’ve been building.

🚨 #Breaking Newz Alert 🚨 "Multiple Shooting Victims Hospitalized After Gunfire Erupts Near Toledo Street Festival" #Ohio  ➡️ A chaotic scene unfolded in Toledo, Ohio, on Saturday afternoon when gunfire erupted near a historic community street festival, leaving multiple individuals wounded & hospitalized. The violent incident drew a massive response from local law enforcement & emergency medical teams who rushed to the neighborhood. ➡️ Emergency dispatchers received the initial reports of a person shot at approximately 5:37 p.m. in the area of Delaware Avenue & Glenwood Avenue. Officers from the Toledo Police Department arrived quickly at the location, which sits right near the active perimeter of the annual Old West End Festival. ➡️ Upon arrival, responding emergency units discovered multiple people suffering from gunshot injuries. First responders quickly began treating victims on the scene & coordinated transport to nearby regional hospitals, though authorities have provided no confirmation on the exact number of people hurt. ➡️ The specific medical conditions of those wounded & the exact motives behind the gunfire remain unverified at this hour. Law enforcement officials have confirmed that no suspects are currently in custody, & investigators are actively searching for the individuals responsible for the violence. ➡️ Local authorities closed down North Detroit Avenue around 5:45 p.m. to establish a secure perimeter & preserve evidence. Detectives are now working to piece together the exact circumstances that led up to the shooting, & they have urged anyone with relevant information to contact the department.

The fraud uncovered in this case makes one thing clear: the real issue is not how to prevent future abuse, but how much has already accumulated over the past two decades. When a network can produce over 100,000 counterfeit certificates, 36,000 fake degrees, and sell them for as little as $1,400, it shows this was never an isolated incident—it was a long‑running, industrialized pipeline. Now that the structure, methods, and participants of this operation have been exposed, the response cannot begin only from today. It must reach backward. The system should re‑examine the past twenty years of visa approvals connected to the same ecosystem, using current evidence and known fraud patterns to reassess old applications, identify those who entered through fabricated credentials or falsified documents, revoke their status, and remove them if necessary. Only by addressing the accumulated stock of past fraud can this visa program return to the purpose it was supposed to serve, instead of remaining vulnerable to exploitation by a well‑organized credential‑forging industry.

Update: Following our initial disclosure, we are sharing additional findings and actions taken regarding the NFT Marketplace exploit investigation.  Security Incident Report — Updated Klever Blockchain — NFT Marketplace Royalty Inflation Exploit Date: June 6, 2026 Status: Mitigated — Investigation Ongoing  Summary  Klever was alerted by the KleverPulse community builder (kpulse.tech) to suspicious on-chain activity. Internal investigation confirmed a critical arithmetic flaw in the NFT Marketplace kApp settlement logic that allowed an attacker to mint KLV out of thin air through self-controlled NFT trades. The fix was deployed to mainnet on June 5, 2026 at 16:00 BRT (fork 19.rc-2). Two epoch transitions have since completed with no anomalies reported.  Exploit Window  2026-06-04T10:38:24Z → 2026-06-05T11:30:32Z (~25 hours)  Root Cause  The settlement layer of the NFT Marketplace kApp failed to validate that the sum of referralPercentage and royalties.marketPercentage could not exceed 100% at the time of the Buy transaction. These values were validated independently and at different points in time, allowing the attacker to manipulate one of them after the sell order was already snapshotted.  At settlement, both the referral payout and the royalty payout were credited in full against the buyer's bid, but the seller's debit was computed as bid − referral − royalty. When both percentages were 100%, this resulted in a negative debit that was silently skipped, effectively minting the difference. Since the attacker controlled the buyer, seller, royalty receiver, and referral address simultaneously, the entire surplus flowed to a single beneficiary.  On-Chain Footprint  The attacker operated across 2 addresses and 283 total transactions, spanning 4 NFT collections (POCDJ08X0X, POCDJ090G7, POCDJ0GG, NFLATION) and 2 marketplaces (both named "Inflation Market"). The buy cycle was executed 60 times, with bid values escalating in a doubling ladder peaking at 25,600,000 KLV per self-buy. A total of 486,357,300.26 KLV was artificially generated by the exploit.  Reconciliation: The attacker's estimated total position (transfers out + DEX swaps + remaining balance) was ~486,527,149 KLV — a delta of ~169,849 KLV, consistent with the attacker's pre-existing legitimate balance and fees consumed during setup operations.  Fund Flow & Exchange Exposure  Post-mint, the attacker dispersed funds through 9 intermediate wallets and routed KLV to at least 4 exchanges, confirmed by taint graph analysis (6 depth levels, 28 tainted edges, 27 chains enumerated):  • KuCoin — ~69,707,673 + ~55,285,585 KLV — Deposited • Bitget — Multiple deposits, traceable — Deposited • Gate — ~777,472 + ~627,135 KLV — Deposited • MEXC — ~792,881 + ~358,471 KLV — Deposited • VoxSwap — ~5,000 KLV — Deposited  Remaining KLV was traced to 10 intermediate wallets still holding funds, flagged for blocking.  On the Ethereum side, fabricated KLV was swapped on the DEX to wrapped assets (USDC, USDT, WBTC, WETH) and bridged out via the ETH–KLV bridge.  Known Attacker Addresses  KLV (primary): klv12n4z3ef86sfk2z97j4fhfta9f2xztsv6frr8faqj7l8q9kc0fcdsfjfqez  ETH: 0x5B9c0b7545aC8a62bff83C73b8A0Ab0568f36E6c 0x163945Fd929c0d4FcE2EA2B6011a789c939cdD59  KLV intermediate wallets flagged for blocking (10 addresses, residues ranging from ~3 KLV to ~55M KLV — full list available on request).  Response & Mitigation  • June 5, 2026 ~16:00 BRT: Fork 19.rc-2 deployed to mainnet. This is the final fix for this vulnerability. • Two epoch transitions completed post-fork with no registered issues. • TRM Labs notified with ETH addresses for blacklisting. • Exchange contacts being pursued for KLV deposit freezes.  Next Steps  • Service Restoration: Gradually restore all affected services, including the ETH–KLV bridge, bitcoin.me, and swap. • Monitoring: Upgrade network monitoring infrastructure with AI-assisted anomaly detection to proactively identify abnormal on-chain behavior across all kApps. • Legal: Initiate formal criminal proceedings, including police reports and applicable legal procedures across relevant jurisdictions, in relation to the exploit. • Buyback Program: Execute a KLV buyback, conducted gradually and at the team's discretion, to counteract the price pressure resulting from the attacker's liquidations. • Token Burn: Plan and execute a burn of the full 486,357,300.26 KLV artificially minted during the exploit, restoring the circulating supply to its pre-attack state. • Complete root cause analysis and publish full post-mortem. • Engage exchanges for account freezes on all confirmed deposit addresses.  This is an updated preliminary report. Information is subject to revision as new findings emerge.

🔥 Pennsylvania Volunteer Firefighter Arrested in Multi‑Fire Arson Case: - A volunteer firefighter in Montgomery County, Pennsylvania — Justin Sholly, 29, of the Perseverance Volunteer Fire Company in Souderton — has been arrested and charged with multiple counts of arson after investigators concluded he set a series of fires and then responded to them with his own department.  🔥 Incident Overview: - Three separate fires occurred within roughly 24–30 hours across Souderton and Franconia Township.   - The fires involved:     - A detached garage and barn near a home with 10 occupants.     - A trash fire later the same day.     - A large multi‑structure blaze early the next morning that engulfed a barn, shed, outhouse, three cars, and a gazebo near a home with eight people inside.    - In total, 18 civilians were placed in danger, though no injuries were reported.   🚓 How Investigators Identified the Suspect: - License plate reader data placed Sholly’s vehicle — a black 2015 Ford Edge — near all three fire scenes.   - A search of his vehicle reportedly uncovered fire starter logs, wood, and a lighter.   - Sholly allegedly admitted to setting all three fires during interviews with detectives.   🚒 Responding to His Own Crimes: - After setting at least two of the fires, Sholly allegedly returned to his fire station and then responded to the scenes with fellow firefighters, participating in suppression efforts.   ⚖️ Motive and Department Response: - Sholly reportedly told investigators that one fire was set near property belonging to a former employer who fired him in 2015.    - The Perseverance Volunteer Fire Company has suspended him with intent to remove, stating that the alleged actions violate the department’s core values.

📕Putin’s Message to Ukraine: “Keep on working, Brothers”📕  Larry C. Johnson  Vladimir Putin used a phrase during the closing session of the Saint Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF) that I think most non-Russians missed or ignored. He said, “Work, Brothers.” First, let me explain the context for Putin saying this.  Zelensky published an open letter to Putin that I, and many others, believe was timed deliberately to coincide with the SPIEF plenary session… This was a provocative move aimed at disrupting the forum’s atmosphere. Putin was asked about it during the question and answer period of the final session. He called the letter “rude” and said it was “no way to set up a face-to-face meeting.” Putin went on to reveal that Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov had tried to show him the letter on two separate occasions — first on June 4, then again just before arriving at SPIEF for his speech this morning (Friday, June 5). He described the letter during his answer in a dismissive way, i.e., he did not think it worthy of a serious response.  Rather than engage with Zelensky’s proposals, Putin turned away from the letter entirely. He said the ones to be addressed were Russia’s combatants and soldiers at the line of contact, telling them:  The country is proud of you and places its hopes on you. We should address not the authors of this letter, nor lovers of the epistolary genre, but our fighters on the front line.  He then closed with the phrase: “Work, brothers!”  To understand the import of that phrase you need to be introduced to Magomed Nurbagandov:  Magomed Nurbagandovich Nurbagandov (January 9, 1985 – July 10, 2016) was a police lieutenant serving in the National Guard of Russia, stationed in Kaspiysk in the Republic of Dagestan. He was a Dargin by nationality, born in the village of Sergokala. By all accounts an exceptional student — he graduated from lyceum with a gold medal and then with honors from the law faculty of Dagestan State University.On the morning of July 10, 2016, Nurbagandov was vacationing with his family near the village of Sergokala when he was attacked by five armed militants. Having learned he was a policeman, the militants forced him and his brother into the trunk of a stolen car, drove them away from the recreation area, and then shot them. The murder was filmed on a mobile phone and posted on an extremist website. WikipediaThe militants’ goal was psychological — they wanted him to appear on camera and call on his fellow officers to quit the police and stop fighting. Instead, looking directly at the camera, Nurbagandov urged: “Keep on working, brothers” (Работайте, братья) — an act which took tremendous courage.The militants had uploaded an edited version of the video where they cut out Nurbagandov’s last words. His defiance was suppressed — until fate intervened. Several militants from the group were killed in September 2016, and when examining the bodies, the mobile phone that had filmed the original, unedited video was found. The full footage — with his final words intact — was then released by Russian authorities. The phrase went viral on September 12, 2016, and became a nationwide sensation.  Since the publication of the unedited video, the phrase “Work, brothers!” has been heard repeatedly on Russian state radio and television, used in media, public speeches, documentary films, appeals, reports, and campaigns. It carries a layered meaning — defiance in the face of death, loyalty to colleagues, and a refusal to be used as a propaganda tool by the enemy. The phrase has since taken on a life beyond the counter terrorism context — used broadly in Russia as an expression of stoic perseverance and professional duty, particularly in military and law enforcement circles.  By invoking it in front of the international audience at SPIEF, Putin was making a layered statement: that Zelensky’s letter was an enemy propaganda exercise, that it deserved to be treated with the same contempt Nurbagandov showed his captors, and that the only people worth addressing are those doing the actual fighting. Putin’s visage was grim when he spoke this phrase.  Shifting topics, I want to comment on the latest exchange of missiles and drones between the US and Iran in the Persian Gulf. Here’s my theory: the Execute Order that the US forces are operating under probably stipulates that their mission is to ensure freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and to identify and destroy communications and weapon systems in the area that the Iranians use to intercept vessels.  Here is the IRGC’s explanation of what happened:  In the name of God, the breaker of the tyrants, So whoever attacks you, attack him in the same way he attacked you.At 01:30 AM today, four violating oil tankers, provoked and directed by the invading US army, attempted to illegally exit the Strait of Hormuz without coordination and without heeding the warnings issued by the IRGC Navy. After the warning, one of the oil tankers was targeted and stopped, and the other violating vessels turned back.Following this incident, at 2 o’clock, American drones struck a communications facility in Qeshm and another in Sirik with two projectiles. In response to the aggression of the child-killing US army, immediately two US airbases in Kuwait named Ali Al-Salem and the important remaining facilities in the US Fifth Fleet naval base in Bahrain were targeted by ballistic missile fire from the IRGC Aerospace Force.  It is possible that the US is using the tankers as bait in order to identify Iranian firing positions and the communications assets used to target the ships trying to sail through the Strait. At this juncture I do not believe that the US will use these encounters as a pretext to go back to a full war footing and launch massive attacks inside Iran. Notwithstanding these maritime and aerial skirmishes, it does appear that serious negotiations are underway. I don’t know if they will succeed, but the US is clearly caught in a “no win” situation.

*** Unanswered Concerns Regarding the BC Conservative Leadership Election ***  Dear BC Conservative MLAs,  I am writing to inform you that I have made repeated attempts over the last two weeks to obtain answers regarding concerns about the recent leadership election. I contacted Board members, the Leadership Election Oversight Committee (LEOC), and all candidates during the leadership race, and received no response.  I was advised by the President of Simply Voting that the Board of Directors requested all questions be directed through them. I was also informed that I would not be receiving the incident report or full documentation, despite the President having previously indicated that a copy of what occurred and was confirmed to have taken place across the election system may be made available. Since then, I have received no follow-up from the Board, the LEOC, or any other party. The help@conservativebc .ca email address has also been closed, further limiting members' ability to raise concerns.  As a result, I will be forwarding all relevant emails and correspondence to each MLA's constituency office for review. I respectfully ask that you examine this matter and take whatever action you deem appropriate.  Party members are entitled to accountability and transparency, particularly when confirmed irregularities in an election process have been acknowledged by the President of Simply Voting. I trust that you take your responsibility to the membership seriously.  Please expect an email with supporting attachments within the next 24 hours.  We Keep Going.

In essence, the fundamental divide is over the identity of the one God whom Christians worship. The Christian God is a single, triune being who is uncreated and eternally God, while the LDS faith teaches of a council of gods, with the Father being an exalted man and Jesus being his firstborn spirit son. This difference reshapes every other core doctrine.  A Christian is a person who believes in and follows the teachings of Jesus Christ as revealed in the Bible. The core doctrines of historical, mainstream Christianity include:  1.  **The Trinity:** God is one eternal being who exists as three co-equal, co-eternal persons: the Father, the Son (Jesus Christ), and the Holy Spirit.  2.  **The Deity of Christ:** Jesus Christ is fully God, eternally begotten of the Father, not a created being. He is one in essence with the Father.  3.  **The Incarnation and Virgin Birth:** God the Son took on human nature, being conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the virgin Mary, remaining fully God and fully man in one person.  4.  **The Atonement:** Christ's death on the cross was a substitutionary sacrifice to satisfy God's justice against sin, thereby reconciling humanity to God. His resurrection conquered death.  5.  **Salvation by Grace Through Faith:** Salvation from sin and its consequences is a gift from God, received by grace alone through faith in Jesus Christ alone, not by human works or merit.  6.  **The Authority of Scripture:** The 66 books of the Old and New Testaments constitute the inspired, inerrant, and sufficient final written revelation of God, forming the sole rule for faith and practice.  The doctrines that set mainstream Christianity apart from the faith of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (LDS) are significant and relate to the very nature of God and the path to salvation:  *   **Nature of God:** Christians affirm the Trinity—one God in three persons. The LDS Church rejects the Trinity, teaching a form of social trinitarianism where the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are three separate and distinct gods. The Father is an exalted man with a physical body, and humans can become gods through exaltation.  *   **Nature of Jesus Christ:** While Christians affirm Jesus is eternally God and uncreated, LDS doctrine teaches Jesus is the firstborn spirit child of God the Father and a heavenly mother, making him a spirit brother to all humans and even to Satan. He became a god, but was not eternally God.  *   **Scripture:** Christians hold the Bible as the sole, final, and sufficient authority. The LDS Church adds to this with other scriptures they consider authoritative, including the Book of Mormon, Doctrine & Covenants, and Pearl of Great Price, which they believe contain further revelation and can supersede the Bible where they conflict.  *   **Salvation:** Christianity teaches salvation is by God's grace through faith in Christ's completed work. The LDS faith teaches a more complex system of salvation that includes grace but requires participation in church ordinances (like baptism and temple endowments) and adherence to a moral code to achieve the highest level of exaltation and godhood.  *   **The Fall:** Traditional Christianity views the Fall of Adam as a catastrophic event that introduced sin and death into God's good creation, necessitating redemption. The LDS faith often frames the Fall as a necessary and fortunate step in human progression, providing a way for spirits to gain physical bodies and experience moral growth.

GROK AI : Hybrid typically does imply (or at least reference) different human species or closely related lineages within the genus Homo. What “Human Hybrids” Means Here An anthropologist saying modern humans are “hybrids” almost certainly refers to archaic admixture—the well-established fact from ancient DNA studies that Homo sapiens (anatomically modern humans) interbred with other hominin groups as they migrated out of Africa.  •  Non-African populations today carry ~1–4% Neanderthal DNA on average. •  Some populations (e.g., Melanesians, some South Asians, Oceanians) also carry Denisovan DNA (up to ~4–6% in certain groups). •  There is also evidence of interbreeding with other “ghost” archaic populations in Africa and elsewhere, plus even a first-generation Neanderthal-Denisovan hybrid child found in fossils.  This is hybridization: successful mating and gene flow between groups that had diverged hundreds of thousands of years earlier.  Does This Mean Different Species? It depends on how strictly you define “species,” but yes in the common paleoanthropological sense: •  Traditional view: Neanderthals (Homo neanderthalensis), Denisovans, and early Homo sapiens are classified as separate species (or sometimes subspecies) within the Homo genus. They were physically and genetically distinct, with separate evolutionary histories after diverging from a common ancestor ~500,000–800,000 years ago. •  Biological species concept (can they produce fertile offspring?): They did interbreed and produce viable, fertile offspring—enough for those archaic genes to persist and even provide adaptive benefits (e.g., immune system advantages, high-altitude adaptations). This blurs strict lines, leading some experts to call them subspecies (Homo sapiens neanderthalensis, etc.) or a “species complex.”  •  Hybridization is common in nature among closely related species (e.g., many mammals, plants), and it doesn’t negate their distinctness if they were mostly separate populations for a long time.  In short: The evidence shows multiple distinct hominin lineages coexisted, met, and mixed. Modern humans are primarily Homo sapiens but with a mosaic genome that includes contributions from these others. We didn’t evolve in complete isolation. Nuance and Ongoing Debate •  Not all humans have the same admixture (sub-Saharan Africans have little to none from Neanderthals/Denisovans, though they may have from other archaic African groups). •  The amount of archaic DNA is small; the vast majority of our genome is Homo sapiens in origin. •  Some popular or fringe discussions exaggerate this into “humans are a hybrid species” for dramatic effect, but mainstream science frames it as limited introgression during replacement/expansion of modern humans.  If the anthropologist meant something more speculative (e.g., older hybridization events or non-standard theories), you’d need more context from the conversation. But the standard scientific meaning aligns with what I described. Human evolution was messier and more interconnected than the old “linear progression” model suggested.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 A darkweb forum post claims that a fresh database belonging to Bank Indonesia, the central bank of the Republic of Indonesia, has been compromised. The listing, published by a user operating under the alias Anonpis, features the title "[ FRESH ] DATABASE BANK INDONESIA" but provides no additional technical details, data samples, or proof of access within the thread.  Bank Indonesia is the nation's central bank, responsible for maintaining monetary stability, issuing the rupiah, and overseeing the country's financial system. As a critical piece of national financial infrastructure, the institution manages vast amounts of sensitive economic data, interbank transaction records, and regulatory information.  If authentic, the exposure of internal central bank databases could potentially enable targeted attacks on Indonesia's financial oversight mechanisms or facilitate large-scale economic espionage.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of Bank Indonesia has not been independently verified, and no corroborating evidence or data dumps have been publicly confirmed.  #BankIndonesia #DataLeak #CentralBank #FinanceSecurity #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity

🇪🇨 🚨 Alleged Compromise of Ecuador's Electoral Registry Database  A threat actor operating under the alias "GordonFreeman" has claimed to have compromised critical systems belonging to Ecuador's National Electoral Council (CNE).  According to the actor, unauthorized access was obtained to multiple devices within the CNE.GOB.EC internal network, leading to full SSH access and the alleged exfiltration of approximately 13.5 million valid voter records from the Electoral Registry database.  The actor further claims that the compromised environment is interconnected with Ecuador's vote transmission and vote-counting infrastructure, potentially enabling unauthorized modifications to voter records, including the addition, alteration, or deletion of voter data.  The alleged breach reportedly affects sensitive electoral information associated with 13,504,210 voter records. Claims also indicate the deployment of persistence mechanisms and backdoors designed to maintain long-term access to affected systems.  #Ecuador #CNE #DataBreach #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ElectionSecurity

Analyst Note:  While 85,000 records may appear small compared to some large-scale breaches, datasets containing accurate identity attributes, contact information, and birth dates often command significant value in underground communities due to their usefulness in fraud, impersonation, and account takeover operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #Switzerland #DarkWeb

Analyst Note:  Large commercial datasets marketed as "business intelligence" often sit in a gray area between publicly aggregated information and improperly collected data. Even when records are business-focused, datasets of this scale can provide valuable reconnaissance material for phishing campaigns, fraud operations, and supply chain targeting.  #DDW #Intelligence #4KKCN #DarkWeb

🇻🇪 Venezuela – INTT (National Institute of Land Transportation) Allegedly Breached  * A threat actor claims to have compromised Venezuela's National Institute of Land Transportation (INTT).  * According to the post, the intrusion was allegedly achieved through an SQL Injection vulnerability on an INTT subdomain.  * The actor claims:   * 788,000 records were extracted   * The source database allegedly contained approximately 8.65 million transportation-related records   * Data extraction was interrupted after the affected system was reportedly taken offline  * The exposed data is described as originating from Venezuela's national transportation and licensing infrastructure.  * If authentic, the compromised information could potentially include:   * Driver license records   * Vehicle registration information   * Citizen identification data   * Transportation-related administrative records   * Personal and contact information  * Government transportation databases are particularly sensitive because they often link identity records, addresses, licensing information, and vehicle ownership details.  * Potential risks include:   * Identity theft   * Fraudulent document creation   * Social engineering campaigns   * Targeted phishing operations   * Criminal intelligence gathering   * Unauthorized profiling of citizens  * At the time of posting, the claims remain unverified and should be treated as allegations until independently confirmed.  Analyst Note:  Transportation registries are high-value targets because they combine verified identity information with vehicle and licensing data. Even partial access can provide threat actors with a highly reliable source of personal information for fraud, impersonation, and intelligence collection activities.  #DDW #Intelligence #INTT #DarkWeb

🇧🇷 Brazil – Creditas Allegedly Breached  * A threat actor claims to have compromised Creditas, one of Brazil's largest fintech and lending platforms.  * The actor alleges access to:   * 85,000+ CPF records (claiming the dataset may exceed 1 million records)   * 170,000+ company CNPJ records   * Mobile phone numbers   * Validated credentials   * Active OAuth tokens   * Payroll-related records   * Reconciliation data   * High-value debtor information   * OTP-related data mappings  * The post further claims exposure of:   * Full names   * Brazilian taxpayer IDs (CPF)   * Loan balances   * Loan status information   * Corporate records   * Financial and payroll data  * The threat actor references alleged vulnerabilities including GraphQL-related issues, IDORs, and SSRF, although no independent verification has been provided.  * If authentic, the exposure could present significant risks including:   * Financial fraud   * Identity theft   * Account takeover   * Loan application fraud   * Targeted phishing campaigns   * Corporate intelligence gathering  * Financial institutions remain high-value targets due to the concentration of sensitive identity, lending, and payment information within their environments.  Analyst Note:  The most concerning aspect of this claim is not the volume of records but the alleged combination of financial profiles, authentication-related data, and validated credentials. If confirmed, this would significantly increase the risk of downstream fraud and account compromise against both individuals and organizations.  #DDW #Intelligence #Creditas #DarkWeb

🇦🇺 Australia – Dymocks Alleged Database Exposure  A threat actor is advertising a database allegedly associated with Dymocks, one of Australia's largest bookstore and retail chains.  * The forum post claims possession of customer-related records from dymocks.com.au and offers the dataset for distribution.  * While the screenshot does not reveal the complete dataset structure, previous Dymocks-related incidents have involved customer information, making independent verification critical before attributing any new exposure.  * If authentic, potential exposed information could enable:   * Credential stuffing attacks   * Targeted phishing campaigns   * Customer impersonation   * Loyalty account abuse   * Identity fraud attempts  * Retail and e-commerce databases remain attractive targets due to the combination of personal information, contact details, purchase history, and loyalty program data.  * Organizations should monitor for:   * Unauthorized account access   * Increased phishing activity targeting customers   * Credential reuse attempts   * Suspicious password reset activity  Analyst Note:  Retail breaches often have a longer operational impact than initially reported. Even when payment card data is absent, customer profiles, email addresses, purchase behavior, and loyalty information can provide threat actors with valuable intelligence for social engineering and fraud operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #Dymocks #DarkWeb

🇦🇷 Argentina – SARHLIQ System Alleged Data Leak  * Threat actor claims to have leaked data from the SARHLIQ System (Sistema SARHLIQ), associated with the Human Resources Secretariat under the Ministry of Labor, Planning, and Human Resources of Catamarca Province.  * According to the post, the exposed records allegedly contain:   * Full names   * National identification numbers (CUIL)   * Gender information   * Dates of birth   * Marital status   * Email addresses   * Physical addresses   * Employment department information   * Withdrawal/termination dates   * Personnel observations and notes  * The threat actor states that full access to the dataset is available and references external distribution channels.  * If authentic, the exposure could affect current and former government employees and may create risks related to identity theft, social engineering, phishing campaigns, and employee profiling.  * Government personnel records are particularly sensitive because they often combine personal identifiers with employment history and organizational information.  Analyst Note:  Human resources databases are frequently targeted because they contain highly structured identity data that can be weaponized for credential attacks, impersonation, targeted phishing, and intelligence collection. Verification of the claim is still required, but the alleged data categories indicate a potentially significant privacy impact.  #DDW #Intelligence #SARHLIQ #DarkWeb

🇺🇦 AlphaSMS Allegedly Breached  Threat actor claims to have compromised AlphaSMS, a Ukraine-based bulk SMS, OTP, and messaging platform.  * The listing advertises a 50.3 GB dataset allegedly containing:   * Customer records   * Payment logs   * Phone logs   * OTP logs   * Viber messages   * WhatsApp messages   * USSD sessions   * SMPP sessions   * Push tokens   * API credentials   * Support tickets   * Operator pricing data  * If verified, the exposure could impact both AlphaSMS and organizations relying on the platform for authentication, messaging, and customer communications.  * The alleged dataset suggests access to operational infrastructure rather than a simple customer database leak.  * Potential risks include:   * Account takeover attempts   * OTP abuse   * API key compromise   * Messaging fraud   * Supply chain exposure   * Intelligence gathering against customers  * Organizations using AlphaSMS should review authentication logs, API credentials, messaging workflows, and any unusual activity involving SMS-based verification.  Analyst Note:  SMS and OTP providers occupy a critical position in modern authentication ecosystems. A compromise of this type can create downstream risks for customers, partners, and end users far beyond the affected provider itself.  #DDW #Intelligence #AlphaSMS #DarkWeb

🇲🇽 Mexican Passport Application Records Allegedly Offered for Sale  * A threat actor is advertising what they claim are Mexican passport application records allegedly sourced from visa and document processing systems.  * According to the listing, each applicant file may contain:   * Passport scans   * CVs and resumes   * University admission documentation   * Financial proof and supporting records   * Health insurance documentation   * Payment receipts   * Additional supporting application documents  * If authentic, this would represent a highly sensitive document exposure rather than a traditional database leak.  * The alleged records could provide threat actors with access to comprehensive identity packages containing multiple forms of verification and supporting documentation.  * Potential risks include:   * Identity theft   * Synthetic identity creation   * Financial fraud   * Account verification abuse   * Immigration and visa fraud   * Social engineering operations   * Document forgery and impersonation schemes  * Unlike typical breaches involving names and email addresses, document collections often contain multiple forms of evidence that can be combined to impersonate individuals across financial, government, and online services.  * Organizations should monitor for:   * Fraudulent identity verification attempts   * Suspicious KYC submissions   * Reuse of passport documents across accounts   * Coordinated identity fraud campaigns   * Underground marketplace activity involving application packages  * Individuals whose records may be affected should remain alert for:   * Unexpected financial activity   * Identity verification requests they did not initiate   * Phishing attempts referencing travel, immigration, or visa matters   * Unauthorized account creation attempts  Analyst Note:  Document-based breaches are often significantly more dangerous than standard credential leaks because they provide threat actors with complete identity portfolios. When passport scans, financial documents, educational records, and supporting paperwork are combined, they can enable sophisticated fraud operations that persist for years after the initial exposure.  #DDW #Intelligence #Mexico #DarkWeb

🇮🇪 AvaTrade Database Allegedly Offered on Underground Forum  * A threat actor has advertised a database allegedly originating from AvaTrade, a global online trading and brokerage platform.  * According to the screenshot, the dataset allegedly contains:   * 221,496 records   * 7 unique data fields  * While the full field list is not visible in the screenshot, databases associated with online trading platforms commonly contain:   * Customer names   * Email addresses   * Phone numbers   * Account identifiers   * Geographic information   * Registration details   * Marketing and customer relationship data  * If authentic, exposure of brokerage-related customer data presents elevated risks because affected users are often targeted by:   * Investment scams   * Cryptocurrency fraud   * Fake broker impersonation campaigns   * Account takeover attempts   * Business email compromise (BEC)   * Social engineering attacks  * Threat actors frequently leverage trading-platform databases to identify individuals with demonstrated interest in financial products and investments.  * Potential impacts include:   * Increased phishing activity targeting traders   * Fraudulent account verification requests   * Investment opportunity scams   * Credential stuffing attacks   * Identity correlation with previously leaked financial datasets  * Financial services organizations should monitor for:   * AvaTrade-themed phishing campaigns   * Unauthorized use of customer information   * Credential exposure linked to brokerage accounts   * Underground marketplace activity involving customer datasets  * At this stage, the forum post should be treated as an unverified claim pending independent validation of the dataset and its origin.  Analyst Note:  Financial-sector datasets are among the most valuable commodities in underground communities because they provide threat actors with pre-qualified targets who are already engaged in trading, investing, or wealth-management activities. Even limited customer information can significantly improve the effectiveness of financial fraud and social engineering operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #AvaTrade #DarkWeb

Always worth remembering that cyber threat actors, even most state-backed ones, generally use tools & techniques just good enough to work against most targets in their target sets. An important implication for defenders: Small but smart changes vs typical can have major benefits.

Well, we finally figured this out. My mom lives in a quadraplex apartment and they've been renovating the building as people moved out these last few years and every time they renovated an apartment they consolidated the power onto her unit, hence it basically quadrupling over the years.  We found out because my sister walked around trying to find the meters and could only find one and then talking to the neighbors and finding out electric was included in their rent. My sister is taking point on talking to the landlord for now to get reimbursed but since that's probably going nowhere it looks like we're getting the utility company and a lawyer involved about theft of services.

I have a quite embarrassing story about being brought in on a weeks long issue, assuming that basic troubleshooting had been done, going down all kinds of rabbit holes, only to reset myself, compare to a known working system, and find that they were pointed at a test environment.

I share this blog with anyone starting out with ASR, as this is the proper way to do it.  I either see environments where everything just stays in Audit Mode and others where they immediately set everything in Block Mode and then we have a mess to comb through.

I think it's a bit of a problem in the spec market of the ten-page rule. It's writing for the reader rather than the movie. The reader doesn't have time to waste on hundreds of bad horror scripts, so they want a promise of premise, threat, and a snapshot of the world and iconography in the first ten, which makes sense for them, but doesn't really make for a good story where you care and are invested. Not to say ten pages without a cold open can't be gripping and well written. Of course, they can, but what gripped me in a theater in the first few scenes of Obsession might not have gripped me in the first ten pages of a read.
If you would have told me in 2016 that computers would be the ones falling for NPM typosquatting in 2026 I would have called you an idiot.

As someone basically birthed with a computer and not much help to get anything to work, and the pride not to ask, the capacity for troubleshooting seems like an innate skill but it simply isn't. And for that reason I'm not sure how to train it.

>multiday user issue in group chat >Cyber is pulled in >please unblock us from the firewall  >show us >user launches web browser to localhost:port >connection refused  >okay sure first check netstat >the port isn't listening >the software isn't running

Defender AV's ASR rules really are fantastic, great way to break attacker tools while still allowing your apps to work  I need to update my blog, but the core is there - KQL queries to help build your allow lists and get it done  nathanmcnulty.com/blog/2022/…  Some lessons learned 🧵
it took me a second.  but…. holy shit.
I love ASR, trying to make it better was one of the last things I did before departing MSFT.   The rules that work properly are S-tier and I 1000% agree that every org should be using them.

Dead serious, I broke a nation-state's kill chain by using the "Block Office from spawning child processes" rule.   I could sense the frustration from the operator on the other side.   Screwing with attackers is honestly a great way to understand what their processes look like and the timelines around them.
Whole-home automation in 1980? There is almost nothing about this online. Steremote  Playboy, November 1980

Active Directory, but it's passively disinterested in its own operational use with almost no scaffolding to demonstrate what successfully deploying it even looks like, so every company on Earth is on a standardized platform for attackers, yet somehow made bespoke for securing it.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-07 08:04 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
생성형 AI를 활용한 공격이 단순 도구 생성을 넘어, 공격 작전 전반을 자동화하고 지능화하는 단계로 진화하고 있습니다. 특히 AI 에이전트와 LLM을 내장한 'AI Worm'의 프로토타입이 등장하며 기업 내부망 침투 및 데이터 탈취의 새로운 위협으로 부상했습니다. 또한, 기존의 전통적인 취약점뿐만 아니라 API 및 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)이 결합된 형태가 빈번하며, CISA 등 주요 기관은 SolarWinds Serv-U와 같은 고위험 취약점의 적극적인 악용 사례를 경고하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Salt Typhoon (NetSecIO):** 미국 통신사를 타겟으로 FBI 감시 네트워크를 침투하여 도청 데이터를 탈취한 것으로 추정되는 중국 연계 그룹.
*   **Hanzala (MEC_Tracker):** PKK 조직을 대상으로 사이버 공격을 수행하여 리더십 정보 및 운영 센터 데이터를 탈취했다고 주장.
*   **Black X (AhnLab ASEC):** 대한민국 내 성형외과를 대상으로 내부 데이터 유출을 시도하는 신규 데이터 강탈 그룹.
*   **Qilin (AhnLab ASEC):** 대한민국 자동화 설비 기업을 대상으로 랜섬웨어 공격 및 데이터 탈취 수행.
*   **VerdantBamboo (dailytechonx):** BRICKSTORM malware를 사용하여 방화벽 및 네트워크 장비를 18개월 이상 은밀하게 점유한 중국계 APT 그룹.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **BGF네트웍스(CU POST):** 외부 해킹 공격으로 인한 고객 개인정보 유출 사고 발생.
*   **자동화 설비 기업 및 성형외과:** Qilin 및 Black X 그룹에 의한 랜섬웨어 및 데이터 유출 공격 타겟팅.
*   **LS ITC & 한국퀀텀컴퓨팅:** 양자내성암호(PQC) 기반 보안 기술 검증을 통해 Agent AI 및 양자 컴퓨터 위협 대응 강화.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-3300:** Everest Forms Pro, WordPress 사이트 완전 제어 가능, **Active Exploitation**.
*   **CVE-2026-20245:** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, Root 권한 획득 가능, **Active Exploitation**.
*   **CVE-2026-28318:** SolarWinds Serv-U, 서버 크래시 유발, **Active Exploitation**.
*   **CVE-2026-0257:** PAN-OS, 방화벽 취약점, **Active Exploitation**.
*   **CVE-2026-49975:** HTTP/2 Bomb, 서버 과부하 유발, **Publicly Disclosed**.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE:**
    *   CVE-2026-3300 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/critical-everest-forms-pro-flaw-exploited-to-take-over-wordpress-sites/))
    *   CVE-2026-0257 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-exploitation-of-pan-os-cve-2026-0257/))
    *   CVE-2026-21404 ([CISA ICS](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-155-01))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **패치 적용:** CISA KEV 목록에 포함된 SolarWinds Serv-U(15.5.4 HF1 이상) 및 Everest Forms Pro 취약점 즉시 패치.
*   **네트워크 장비 점검:** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN 장비의 비정상적인 설정 변경 여부 모니터링 및 관리자 계정 접근 제어 강화.
*   **공급망 보안:** npm 등 오픈소스 의존성 관리 도구를 활용하여 프로젝트 내 취약한 패키지 식별 및 제거.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8.5/10
*   **근거:** 다수의 고위험 취약점이 실제 악용(Active Exploitation) 중이며, AI 기반 공격 도구의 확산으로 인해 방어 체계의 복잡성이 급격히 증가함.
